Okay, so one of the things that we heard in your kickoff call was that you're getting more interest from SMBs and obviously we're.
Exploring a lot of content opportunities related to small to medium-sized businesses, there's obviously going to be a big difference in the way that content speaks to these different audiences.
So are you already thinking about how content could serve both enterprise and SMBs?
Are you making a difference between them or are you planning to target them sort of with a holistic strategy?
No, we are definitely going to target them separately.
They have different pain points, different concerns.
They're, you know, and enterprise is always going to be at a different level of governance maturity than small or medium businesses.
So, you know, there's no like one size fits all approach that we're going to take.
However, we do have some materials on the enterprise side that have historically worked well.
All of it are probably low-hanging fruit for updating and adapting for small and medium businesses.
So that is definitely an opportunity that we would want to pursue.
Can you give me some examples of what has worked well for enterprises?
Yeah, I think I mentioned this on the kickoff call.
We've historically found that content around the corporate secretary's job and what they do and what their concerns are is high-performing for us.

We've also had some similar results with doing content around like a general counsel's end-of-year checklist and things like that that help people do their jobs better and show how technology enables them to do their jobs better and specifically how diligent technology really helps them do that.
Small and medium businesses will likely have somebody serving as a general counsel or a chief legal officer.
So they probably do not yet have a corporate secretary.
That's something you start putting in place as you think about going public.
But off the top of my head, those are a couple of the things that have tended to resonate well.
We've also historically done pretty well with things that are like templates for how to take minutes, templates for how to use technology to take minutes at your meetings.
People want to know how to do things.

On our mission-driven organization side of the house, where we, by nature, target a lot of small organizations just because we're going after nonprofits and local governments and school boards, there's a lot of interest in things like Roberts Rules of Order and parliamentary procedures.
So there's almost like this baseline layer of knowledge that people are looking for that I think we could be exploring more.
I think one thing that we haven't necessarily nailed, and stop me if I'm going into too much detail that you don't No, absolutely not, yeah.
One thing that I don't 100% know if we've nailed is, do we have the right story for why an SMB should care about getting their governance and order?
And this year, historically, we've done all that through an IPO angle.
Which is, you know, if you're getting ready to go public, you've got to have everything in order before you do that and got to get good governance in order ahead of time.
And having good governance before you go public tends to enable you to outperform your competition that doesn't have it in order ahead of time.
With the IPO market as nuts as it has been in recent years, that message doesn't really work as well.
So, this year, we've started testing out, like, transaction-ready platform.

Um...

think most recently we're going with transaction ready, but it's before you do any sort of merger or acquisition, before you try to raise your next round of funding, before you look for an exit, whether that's an acquisition or going public, make sure you're transaction ready by having all your governance and risk management in place ahead of time.
And I don't know if we've had that out there long enough to really know if it's working yet.

So that's a really long process, and I know there's a lot of interest in shortening that to just two calls.

So I think in the, when we're in the kickoff call, you mentioned that, or someone on the team mentioned that a business can start small and grow with diligence.
So we would just like to get more.
And how does that work?
So I know that in the demo, for example, they work with someone to personalize the demo to their needs.
So how does that work on the product side?
Like if an SMB signs up with Diligent, how can they start small?
Yeah.
So if they're coming to us for a governance solution and they're small, chances are high that they don't have any solution in place.
So they're not trying to replace something.

They're trying to fix what's not working for them now, which is usually a very manual, time-consuming process where whoever is administering their board meetings, if they're even having board meetings, they're doing that with spreadsheets and collecting information for the board books from a lot of different sources.
And there's issues with version control and getting their information into the hands of their directors in a timely fashion.
And last minute updates for the board meeting through our...
Everything out of whack.
They're struggling to actually run the meetings effectively, especially in a post-COVID world where people could be anywhere.

They're struggling to get their leadership and directors to vote on actions, and they're struggling to get their meetings or their minutes created and those minutes memorialized and approved and distributed.
So it's all very manual if they're coming to us for a governance solution as a small company.

So we would start small by giving them a very sort of light governance package that doesn't necessarily have all the bells and whistles, but does help them digitize that process and gives them much more control over what's going into the board book and helping them do that.
As they continue to scale and grow over time, we have additional governance features and functionality that helps them really grow in that governance space and helps their spend.
Diligent grow, but we also start adding in risk product functionality, compliance product functionality.
As companies get bigger, their needs change, their reporting needs change, their regulatory requirements change.
They need to start paying attention to risk audit and compliance in a way that they might not be as a small business.
So we're there to serve them with those things as they grow and expand.
Okay, that's super helpful to know.
Just to confirm, so board and governance, that is the priority solution for to spotlight in the content and for the content strategy.
Please correct me if I'm wrong.
Yes, yes, yeah.
So, yeah, the governance products.
So we don't really call it board and governance or boards and governance name, but yeah.
Those are all names.

third email, to attention.
